@@ -220,6 +220,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l0mb","title":"acfs zsh function: support 'session' subcommand","description":"In acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc, the acfs() function dispatches subcommands but omits 'session', even though scripts/lib/doctor.sh implements 'acfs session'. This causes to fail in zsh because the function shadows the installed acfs CLI. Add a session case (and update help text) to forward to doctor.sh session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T22:35:08.936641-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T22:36:50.456073-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T22:36:50.456073-05:00","close_reason":"acfs() zsh shim now forwards to doctor.sh session; validated zsh syntax."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","title":"Task: Create Troubleshooting page for common issues","description":"","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:01.286219-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:30:18.130145-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:30:18.130145-05:00","close_reason":"Enhanced troubleshooting page with 5 new issues: Host Key Verification Failed, Too Many Auth Failures, Slow Connection, VPS Provider Verification. Page already existed with solid foundation - added missing content from ssh_troubleshooting.md.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:57.855209-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l2w","title":"Harden sync_ntm_palette.sh: HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing","description":"scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh currently uses plain curl -fsSL and may fetch the remote file twice when computing the hash. Align with scripts/lib/security.sh (HTTPS-only curl args + enforce_https) and compute remote hash from a single fetch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:46:02.630169-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","close_reason":"Resolved on main: sync_ntm_palette.sh now uses scripts/lib/security.sh for HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing (commit d683f1f). Shellcheck clean.","labels":["scripts","security"]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l334","title":"services-setup.sh: allow --help without resolving TARGET_HOME","description":"`./scripts/services-setup.sh --help` should work without requiring target-user home resolution. Today the script resolves `TARGET_HOME` and exits early if it can't (which can happen in non-target environments or if TARGET_USER is unknown), preventing `--help` from ever printing. Fix: - Defer `TARGET_HOME`/`BUN_BIN` initialization until `main()` (and until `--install-claude-guard` action). - Keep `--help` action working without initialization. Quality gates: - `shellcheck scripts/services-setup.sh` - `bash scripts/services-setup.sh --help` ","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:06:08.318027-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T00:06:18.627625-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l4eu","title":"Harden report_failure_json JSON output","description":"report_failure_json() uses jq --argjson for phase numbers and manual fallback JSON that doesn't escape some string fields; harden numeric handling and ensure fallback JSON is valid/escaped to avoid breaking error reporting under set -e.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T15:00:59.077932-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T15:02:51.018641-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T15:02:51.018641-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed report_failure_json to normalize numeric fields (null if unknown), avoid jq --argjson aborts, and escape all string fields in fallback JSON."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l82e","title":"Task: Add installation output interpretation guide","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:20.770441-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.950908-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.950908-05:00","close_reason":"Addressed in r1z5 and existing run-installer page content: SSH disconnect warning (already present), installation output interpretation (added), curl command explanation (already present)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l82e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:54.359928-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lbo","title":"doctor: include user tool dirs in PATH","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh relies on PATH-only lookups, so running `acfs doctor` in a non-zshrc environment (fresh ssh, bash shell, etc.) can falsely report missing tools that are installed under ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, etc. Make doctor self-contained by prepending common ACFS install dirs to PATH before checks (and optionally improve version detection for commands like go/tmux).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:46:21.82316-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","close_reason":"doctor self-contained PATH + version handling"} @@ -359,6 +360,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-x01y","title":"TASK: Implement acfs dashboard serve command","description":"# TASK: Implement acfs dashboard serve command ## Context Create a command that serves the dashboard via a temporary HTTP server. ## Implementation ### Command ```bash acfs dashboard serve [--port 8080] ``` ### Function ```bash acfs_dashboard_serve() { local port="${1:-8080}" local dashboard_dir="${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/dashboard" # Generate if missing if [[ ! -f "${dashboard_dir}/index.html" ]]; then acfs_dashboard_generate fi # Get IP for display local ip=$(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}') # Show banner cat < = { vps: "Virtual Private Server - a cloud computer...", ssh: "Secure Shell - encrypted connection...", // ... more terms }; ``` ### Proposed Data Structure ```typescript // lib/glossary.ts export interface GlossaryTerm { term: string; definition: string; category: 'concepts' | 'tools' | 'protocols' | 'acronyms'; relatedTerms?: string[]; learnMoreUrl?: string; } export const GLOSSARY: GlossaryTerm[] = [ // Concepts { term: 'agentic', definition: 'Software that uses AI agents to autonomously perform tasks...', category: 'concepts' }, { term: 'flywheel', definition: 'A self-reinforcing loop that gains momentum...', category: 'concepts' }, // Tools { term: 'tmux', definition: 'Terminal multiplexer that allows multiple terminal sessions...', category: 'tools' }, { term: 'ntm', definition: 'Named Tmux Manager - a wrapper around tmux...', category: 'tools' }, { term: 'ripgrep', definition: 'Ultra-fast recursive grep alternative...', category: 'tools' }, // Protocols { term: 'ssh', definition: 'Secure Shell - encrypted protocol for remote access...', category: 'protocols' }, { term: 'oauth', definition: 'Open Authorization - protocol for delegated authentication...', category: 'protocols' }, // Acronyms { term: 'vps', definition: 'Virtual Private Server - a cloud-hosted virtual machine...', category: 'acronyms' }, { term: 'cli', definition: 'Command Line Interface - text-based interface...', category: 'acronyms' }, { term: 'api', definition: 'Application Programming Interface - way for programs to communicate...', category: 'acronyms' }, ]; export function getTermByName(name: string): GlossaryTerm | undefined { return GLOSSARY.find(t => t.term.toLowerCase() === name.toLowerCase()); } ``` ## Page Design ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Glossary │ │ │ │ [🔍 Search terms...] │ │ │ │ [All] [Concepts] [Tools] [Protocols] [Acronyms] │ │ │ │ A │ │ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ agentic │ │ Software that uses AI agents to autonomously... │ │ Related: flywheel, ntm │ │ │ │ api │ │ Application Programming Interface - way for... │ │ │ │ F │ │ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │ │ flywheel │ │ A self-reinforcing loop that gains momentum... │ │ Related: agentic │ │ [Learn more in Lesson 7 →] │ │ ... │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Features 1. **Alphabetical grouping** - Terms organized A-Z 2. **Category tabs** - Filter by type 3. **Search** - Quick find by term 4. **Related terms** - Cross-links between terms 5. **Deep links** - `/learn/glossary#ssh` jumps to term ## Refactoring Jargon Component Update `components/jargon.tsx` to use the shared data: ```typescript import { getTermByName } from '@/lib/glossary'; export function Jargon({ term }: { term: string }) { const termData = getTermByName(term); if (!termData) return {term}; return ( {term}

{termData.definition}

 ); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page accessible at `/learn/glossary` - [ ] All jargon terms from webapp are included - [ ] Alphabetical grouping works - [ ] Category filtering works - [ ] Search filters in real-time - [ ] Jargon component refactored to use shared data - [ ] Deep links work (e.g., `/learn/glossary#ssh`) ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/lib/glossary.ts` - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/glossary/page.tsx` - Update: `apps/web/components/jargon.tsx` (refactor)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:22:52.194711-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:22.86564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:22.86564-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented /learn/glossary using lib/jargon terms with search, category filter, A–Z grouping, deep links, and related-term anchors.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-xot","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1y8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.785578-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-xv9e","title":"TASK: Implement cheatsheet terminal renderer","description":"# TASK: Implement cheatsheet terminal renderer ## Context Render the parsed aliases as a beautiful, categorized cheatsheet in the terminal. ## Implementation ### Function ```bash cheatsheet_render() { local filter="${1:-}" # Get parsed aliases local aliases aliases=$(cheatsheet_parse_aliases) # Group by category # Render with gum/plain formatting # Apply filter if provided } ``` ### Features - Categorized sections with headers - Two-column layout (alias → description) - Color coding by category - Search/filter by pattern - Gum-enhanced with fallback ### Search Mode ```bash acfs cheatsheet git # Filter to git category acfs cheatsheet "push" # Search for 'push' in name/desc ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Categorized output - [ ] Filterable by category or search term - [ ] Readable on 80-column terminal - [ ] Gum-enhanced with plain fallback - [ ] Shows description/expansion for each alias","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:58:32.632274-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:12:03.093174-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:12:03.093174-05:00","close_reason":"Complete: cheatsheet.sh with dynamic alias parsing, terminal/JSON output, filtering, integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-xv9e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-thvw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:58:55.542635-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
